regression_check: POST /api/workspaces with explicit repo_url must persist that exact value on the workspace row AND render it into agent templates at create-time; omitting the field must continue to inherit the GitHub integration snapshot (backwards-compat).
reviewer_notes: Three observations for the reviewer. (1) The Wave-1 bug write-up framed the fallback as the 'OPENSEC_REPO_URL env var', but in the live code the fallback is the GitHub integration's config.repo_url — which is later exported AS OPENSEC_REPO_URL to the OpenCode subprocess via _resolve_repo_env_vars. Semantically the same 'global default', preserved unchanged for the no-field path. (2) AC3 (CONTEXT/agent templates reference the explicit URL) is satisfied by passing repo_url into write_agents at create-time. This required adding **extra kwargs to AgentTemplateEngine.write_agents and render_all so the value forwards into render_agent (which already accepted **extra). The executor's run-time re-render path (executor.py:677) is unchanged and uses the same value via OPENSEC_REPO_URL. (3) Branch landed on claude/elated-murdock-fdcba2 (worktree branch off main's tip 289954c, matches the #157/#158/#159 single-PR merge pattern) rather than the shared qa/q01-campaign-fixes branch — that branch is checked out at the project root with unrelated WIP (09ae135 is an OpenRouter slug fix, not Wave-1 blocker work), and stepping on it would risk colliding with parallel sessions.
